title: [main.c:128] ambiguous return value on goto cleanup path
description: At line 128, when goto cleanup is reached (eui64 == NULL), the return is '(ret < 0) ? ret : -ENODATA'. But ret is never set to negative before the goto at line 108. ret is only set by init/start calls (lines 91, 99), not by get_eui64. So this will always return -ENODATA, making the ternary redundant. Fix: simplify to 'return -ENODATA;' or set ret = -ENODATA before goto.
close_reason: Fixed: set ret = -ENODATA before goto cleanup, eliminating redundant ternary
